converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Advocate Research and Education Foundation records
Advocate Research and Education Foundation
7310
12.5 cubic feet. 12.5 cubic feet.
1978-1981. Correspondence, financial records, registration files, topical seminar background material, "facilitator information sheets," audio and video cassettes, slides. The collection pertains chiefly to the Advocate Experience, an EST-derived seminar for lesbians and gays founded by David B. Goodstein. The contains various materials from 1978 to 1981 of the Advocate Research and Education Foundation Board and the Advocate Experience staff, and manuals pertaining to the Advocate Experience and the Outrageous Experience (a seminar for instructors). Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 14 ======= 14 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2086576
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Phil McCray
Date completed:
September 1991
EAD encoding:
Mireille Lee, April 2000
ORGANIZATIONAL HISTORY
In the 1970s, David B. Goodstein created the Advocate Research and Education Foundation, a non-profit structure through which to conduct the "Advocate Experience." Initially the Experience was a one-weekend seminar similar to Werner Erhard's EST, only targeted at a gay audience. The foundation was eventually combined with the Mariposa Education and Research Foundation.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
The collection is divided into four series: Advocate Research and Education Foundation and Advocate Experience Correspondence and Memos' and Alphabetical Subject File; Facilitator Information Sheets; and an Audio and Video cassette series. The Facilitator Information Sheets are restricted until 2041.
RELATED MATERIAL
David B. Goodstein papers, #7311.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Correspondence (incoming and outgoing)
Boxes 1-4
Series II. Alphabetical Subject File
Boxes 5-9
Series III. Advocate Experience Facilitator Information Sheets
Boxes 10-12
Series IV. Advocate Experience Audio and Video Cassettes and Slides
Box 13
Cite As:
Advocate Research and Education Foundation/Advocate Experience Records, #7310. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Access Restrictions:
Access to Boxes 10-12 restricted until 2041
Est, an Educational Corporation. Psychotherapy. Gays. Gay men. Homosexuality. Goodstein, David B., 1932-

Access restrictedSeries IV. Advocate Experience Audio and Video Cassettes and SlidesSixteen videotapes pertaining to the Advocate Experience, including training sessions, graduates sharing the experience, and David B. Goodstein presentations13Twenty audiocassettes pertaining to the Advocate Experience, including seminar sessions, Opening Space session, disco music, and "Ethel Merman Single"13Approximately 90 slides pertaining to the Advocate Experience13 ======= Series I.
